name: Uncommon Process Spawned from HWP
notes:
This is rule detects uncommon processes spawning from Hangul Word Processor (Hanword).

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Suspicious HWP Sub Processes
SIGMA Description: Detects suspicious Hangul Word Processor (Hanword) sub processes that could indicate an exploitation


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") == 'hwp.exe' AND LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE '%gbb.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Uncommon Process Spawned from HWP on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") == 'hwp.exe' AND LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE '%gbb.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Uncommon Process Spawned from HWP
Event description: Detects uncommon processes spawning from Hangul Word Processor (Hanword).
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
